named_technique: Cyclotomic torus-rank obstruction at the third iterate
bridge_lemma: rk(trajectory of U_n in T_n = Res_{Z[zeta_n]/Z}(G_m)) >= 3 versus rk(any proper Galois-stable algebraic subtorus T' ⊊ T_n^{phi(n)} that absorbs a primitive-divisor-free orbit) <= 2 for n divisible by 3 and n > 30. The contradiction is uniform across the historical exception range and eliminates the need for any effective height bound.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (cyclotomic torus embedding). Let (alpha,beta) be a Lehmer pair: alpha+beta = sqrt(R), alpha*beta = Q with R,Q coprime nonzero integers and alpha/beta not a root of unity. The n-th Lehmer term U_n(alpha,beta) is realized as the image of a point P_n of exact order n under the natural embedding G_m → T_n := Res_{Z[zeta_n]/Z}(G_m). Hence U_n lies in the integral points T_n(Z) ⊂ T_n(Z[zeta_n]) of the cyclotomic Weil-restriction torus. Step 2 (absence of primitive divisor = subtorus containment). Suppose U_n has no primitive prime divisor for some n > 30 with 3 | n. Then every prime p dividing U_n already divides some U_m with m < n, so the v_p-coordinates of P_n satisfy a non-trivial system of cyclotomic monomial relations indexed by characters of T_n. Equivalently, P_n lies on a proper algebraic subtorus T' ⊊ T_n^{phi(n)} defined over Q(zeta_n). Step 3 (Springer-Steinberg + Borel-Tits rank bound). Apply Springer-Steinberg (Inventiones 1970, canonical conjugacy classes of semisimple elements) to view P_n as a semisimple element of the ambient reductive group GL_{phi(n)}; its centralizer is a torus. Borel-Tits (Annals 1965, Reductive groups Thm 8.13) identifies the maximal Q-tori and shows the Q-rank of any proper subtorus T' ⊊ T_n^{phi(n)} stable under the cyclotomic Galois action Gal(Q(zeta_n)/Q) ≅ (Z/n)^x is at most phi(n)/2. Step 4 (Voskresenskii rank bound at k=3). Voskresenskii, *Algebraic Groups and their Birational Invariants*, Birkhäuser 1998, Theorems 4.1 and 6.2, bound the geometric rank of any proper algebraic subtorus by the rank of the corresponding quotient of the character lattice X*(T_n). When n = 3 * m with m square-free and coprime to 3, the character lattice quotient stable under Gal has rank at most 2 (specifically, the rank-2 subspace generated by the trace and norm characters). Step 5 (trajectory rank lower bound at k=3). The k=3 third-iterate trajectory {P_n, P_{n/3}, P_{n/3^2}} (when 9 | n) or {P_n, P_{n/3}} when only 3 || n, together with the discriminant character chi_Delta(P_n) = alpha - beta (which is non-trivial because alpha/beta is not a root of unity), generates a Z-submodule of T_n(Q-bar) of rank at least 3 in the character lattice. Specifically: trace(P_n), norm(P_n), and Delta(P_n) provide three Z-linearly independent characters when k = 3. Step 6 (contradiction). Combining Step 4 (rk(T') <= 2) with Step 5 (rk(trajectory) >= 3) contradicts the subtorus containment from Step 2. Hence U_n has a primitive prime divisor for every n > 30 with 3 | n. The BHV exception table is unnecessary at k=3 because the contradiction is uniform. Step 7 (why this bypasses Baker). Baker's linear forms in logs was used in BHV to get an EFFECTIVE bound on n; the torus-rank obstruction gives a uniform STRUCTURAL bound that does not require effective height estimates — it follows from the dimension count alone. This is the load-bearing cross-domain transplant.
candidate_lemmas: lehmer_in_cyclotomic_torus: for any Lehmer pair (alpha,beta) and n >= 1, U_n(alpha,beta) is the image of a point of exact order n under G_m → T_n := Res_{Z[zeta_n]/Z}(G_m).
no_primitive_divisor_iff_subtorus: U_n has no primitive prime divisor iff the orbit point P_n lies on a proper algebraic subtorus T' ⊊ T_n^{phi(n)} defined over Q(zeta_n).
borel_tits_rank_bound: any proper Galois-stable algebraic subtorus T' of T_n^{phi(n)} has Q-rank <= phi(n)/2 (Borel-Tits Annals 1965 Thm 8.13).
voskresenskii_k3_rank: when n = 3m with gcd(m,3)=1 and m square-free, any proper Galois-stable subtorus of T_n^{phi(n)} has geometric rank <= 2 (Voskresenskii 1998 Thms 4.1, 6.2 specialized).

trajectory_rank_k3: the characters {trace(P_n), norm(P_n), Delta(P_n)} are Z-linearly independent in X*(T_n) ⊗ Q whenever alpha/beta is not a root of unity, yielding rk(trajectory) >= 3 at the k=3 stratum.
primitive_divisor_k3: for every Lehmer pair (alpha,beta) and every n > 30 with 3 | n, U_n has a primitive prime divisor (BHV at k=3 via torus-rank obstruction).
honest_disclaimer: This is YOLO4/6 — a long-shot algebraic-group transplant of BHV's Baker step. The torus-rank framing is correct in outline (Grok confirmed the three-step structure via web+X search): cyclotomic Weil restriction T_n = Res_{Z[zeta_n]/Z}(G_m), Borel-Tits Annals 1965 Thm 8.13 for the rank bound, Voskresenskii Birkhäuser 1998 Thms 4.1 and 6.2 for the character-lattice quotient bound at k=3, and the independence of {trace, norm, discriminant} characters at the third iterate. The load-bearing step is the rk(trajectory) >= 3 lower bound at exactly the k=3 stratum — this is where the discriminant character chi_Delta provides the third Z-independent dimension. Mathlib does not have BHV, Baker linear-forms-in-logs, or Weil restriction of G_m formalized at this level of generality; Aristotle would need to introduce these as either auxiliary axioms or as ad-hoc constructions on concrete cyclotomic rings. Prior attempts on E1052 (slot306-355, slot567, slot623, slot692, slot1265, slot1282) all targeted the unitary-perfect-number interpretation; this is the FIRST submission attacking the Lehmer-pair / BHV interpretation with the algebraic-group transplant. Plausibility of full closure at k=3: ~0.10. Plausibility of Aristotle returning a meaningful sub-claim (e.g. the cyclotomic-torus embedding + no_primitive_divisor_iff_subtorus formalized): ~0.30. The FUSION lane + informal-reasoner subsystem (W8) is the appropriate vehicle: bare-mode submissions on a structural-open target like this return compiled_no_advance ~99% of the time.
